description: Intentional divergences from stock SUSHI v3.20.0, where stock produces INVALID or clearly-buggy output and we deliberately produce correct output instead. Tracked, counted separately from byte-parity (NOT as failures). The mechanism is DIFF-BASED, not file-based: each entry pins the EXACT expected `diff <stock> <ours>` in tests/compat-golden/<ig>/<file>.diff. Each gate run recomputes the stock-vs-ours diff and asserts it EQUALS the recorded diff, so the ONLY tolerated difference is the specific one recorded; ANY other change in that file (a regression, or a new divergence) makes the diff differ and is flagged UNEXPECTED. If the diff becomes empty (stock now matches ours), the entry is RESOLVED/obsolete and should be removed. Re-record after an intended change by regenerating the .diff.

divergences.reason: Stock emits an empty required-extension scaffold {url:.../extension-authorizedProviderType} with no value[x] and no sub-extensions, violating FHIR invariant ext-1. It is an order-dependent side-effect of stock's shared InstanceExporter.sdCache. Stock build is silent (0 errors/0 warnings). We emit valid FHIR (omit the empty scaffold).
Same ext-1 violation (empty required authorizedProviderType extension scaffold). See ClaimResponse-RejectionResponseExample.
Bundle embedding the above ClaimResponse; same ext-1 violation in the contained resource.
Bundle embedding the PractitionerRequestor ClaimResponse; same ext-1 violation in the contained resource.
